reviews.review_msg: Sam is an average professor, but not the worst. For the final exam, we've been given no information about what to expect or how it will be structured. This has made it very difficult to study, since I'm unsure what to focus on. At this point, it feels like the exam will be a complete surprise, and I don't know what to expect.

Sam was not ideal, he was not the best but I have definitely had worse, refusing to give the class any information about the final exam is an interesting choice, and lectures were not well explained and he often would not know the answer to peoples questions. I found the content to be interesting enough but more real world context would have helped

I wish we had Sam for the entire semester. I was unfortunately only able to go to half his lectures but when I did go he explained things very well. There are a lot of complex processes in his part of the course but he taught them well. His questions were hard but super fair, he didn't throw any curveballs like you see in other BPK courses.
Good professor. Teaches very well and his testing makes sense. I wish he taught the whole course instead of just the second half.

The worst prof for this class. He just reads off his slides and can't answer any basic questions about diagrams or words that are straight from his slides. It's like he didn't make them or even read them before class. Class usually ends early with him but its because he doesn't go into any detail that you need for exams and skims everything.

His lectures are ok. The problem was the questions on the exam. It felt very lackluster and felt that very little effort was put in for the exam. The difference in quality between Nadine's exam questions and Sam's is very clear. If he is teaching for the semester, it would be a good idea to skipped that term.
Good prof. Exams questions match the lectures but are more like 305 than 306. Go to lectures and to office hours.

Dr. Doesburg is very informative and I enjoyed his lectures. The second half of this course is very complicated so I don't think that's his fault. His pacing is good, it leaves me enough time to jot down important notes. Whenever I ask him a question, he answers directly and clearly which I appreciate!

Reads off the slides. Im basically falling asleep in class. He doesnt really explain topics that well. He goes off topic quite often. Also talks very quick which makes it hard to get notes down and we get off class 8-10 mins earlier than normal. Wished he would use that time to break down more complex topics.
